heroHeadline: Israeli Army Fires on WHO Vehicle in Southern Gaza, Killing One
heroSubtitle: Israeli forces opened fire on a clearly marked WHO humanitarian vehicle in southern Gaza on April 6, 2026, killing one person according to medics — marking a direct strike on a protected humanitarian convoy. Ben-Gvir's Palestinian 'death row' proposal continues to draw international condemnation. Gaza Phase 2 ceasefire negotiations remain frozen.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, IDF, Gaza Ministry of Health, B'Tselem, ICC, ICJ, Reuters, Al Jazeera, OHCHR, UNRWA, Yesh Din, Human Rights Watch, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are subject to ongoing verification. The conflict has persisted since Israeli independence on May 14, 1948.
